text.title: mobile-responsive Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules for mobile-first responsive web CSS.

rule.statement: Use `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">` on every page that serves mobile.
rule.rationale: Without viewport meta, mobile browsers render at 980px and scale down — text becomes unreadable, layout breaks.

rule.statement: Write mobile-first CSS: base styles target 320px; use `min-width` media queries to enhance for larger viewports.
rule.rationale: Mobile-first produces simpler code; desktop-first accumulates override debt at small viewports.

rule.statement: Use `clamp(min, preferred, max)` for fluid typography and spacing. Avoid fixed px values above 1px (borders excepted).
rule.rationale: clamp() scales fluidly across viewports; fixed px values force breakpoint stacking.

rule.statement: Tap targets must be at least 44x44px (WCAG 2.5.5 and Apple HIG).
rule.rationale: Smaller targets cause mis-taps; a11y audit failures; mobile UX score penalty.

rule.statement: On iOS Safari, use `100dvh` (or `svh`/`lvh`) instead of `100vh`. The 100vh value includes the URL bar.
rule.rationale: 100vh on iOS hides bottom UI behind the URL bar; dvh/svh/lvh respect the actual visible viewport.

rule.statement: No hover-only interactions. Every hover affordance must have a tap-equivalent (long-press, focus, or always-visible variant).
rule.rationale: Touch devices do not fire hover; hover-only features are invisible to mobile users.

rule.statement: Run Playwright at 360/768/1280/1920 viewports + Lighthouse mobile audit; no UI ships without screenshots at all four widths.
rule.rationale: Eyeballing on a 27" monitor misses mobile regressions; mechanical verification catches them.

rule.statement: Viewport meta + mobile-first + clamp() + 44px taps + dvh + no hover-only + multi-viewport Playwright pass.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the positive branch.

rule.statement: Not a web UI, or audience is fixed-viewport-only.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion for the negative branch.
